name: Decadent Chocolate Cherry Fruitcake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
2. Grease and flour a 6- or 12-cup Bundt pan or a 10-inch tube pan.
3. In a large bowl, combine water, kirsch, vegetable oil, and egg. Mix until well combined.
4. Add the Nut bread mix and pecans to the bowl. Stir until all ingredients are just mixed together.
5. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and spread evenly.
6. Bake in the preheated oven for 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) for 25 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
7. Remove from the oven and let cool in the pan for about 25 minutes.
8. Loosen the edges of the cake with a knife, then carefully remove it from the pan.
9. Cool the cake completely on a wire rack.
10. Wrap the cooled cake tightly in plastic wrap or foil and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks, or freeze for up to 3 months.
11. Just before serving, melt the chocolate chips and vegetable oil together in a small saucepan over low heat (or in a microwave), stirring until smooth.
12. Drizzle the melted chocolate mixture over the cooled fruitcake.
Note: The cake can also be baked in a 9- by 5-inch loaf pan for 60 to 70 minutes. To double the recipe, bake for 70 to 85 minutes in a 12-cup Bundt pan or a 10-inch tube pan, or for 60 to 70 minutes in two 9-inch loaf pans.
